proposal_text: This research proposes to develop a machine learning model to optimise hospital bed allocation across three Dublin hospitals. The model will predict patient length of stay, readmission risk, and discharge timing using electronic health records including diagnosis codes, vital signs, medication history, and demographic information including age, gender, and medical card status. The training dataset consists of 80,000 anonymised patient records from 2018-2024. The model will generate daily recommendations for bed managers on patient transfers and early discharge candidates. The researchers acknowledge that medical card status (a proxy for socioeconomic deprivation) could influence the model's predictions in ways that disadvantage lower-income patients. The model outputs will be advisory but will be integrated into the hospital's electronic patient record system. No mechanism for clinical override documentation has been designed.

assessment.risk_summary: The proposed research involves the development of a machine learning model that processes sensitive patient data, including diagnosis codes, vital signs, and demographic information. The model's predictions may disadvantage lower-income patients due to medical card status being used as a proxy for socioeconomic deprivation. Additionally, the integration of the model's outputs into the hospital's electronic patient record system raises concerns about clinical override documentation.

identified_risks.risk: Bias in data and model outputs
identified_risks.explanation: The use of medical card status as a proxy for socioeconomic deprivation may lead to biased predictions that disadvantage lower-income patients.

The integration of the model's outputs into the hospital's electronic patient record system without a mechanism for clinical override documentation raises concerns about accountability and transparency.
The processing of sensitive patient data, including diagnosis codes, vital signs, and demographic information, requires careful consideration of data protection and privacy regulations.
The integration of the model's outputs into the hospital's electronic patient record system may introduce security vulnerabilities that need to be addressed.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Be fair and take action not to discriminate — consider and ameliorate biases the approach may perpetuate
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Conduct a thorough analysis of the data and model outputs to identify and mitigate any biases.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Consider and ameliorate biases in data and biased code/model outputs
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Develop strategies to address the potential biases in the data and model outputs.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Respect privacy — obtain informed consent where required and protect personally identifiable information
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Ensure that all necessary measures are taken to protect the privacy of patients and their data.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Design and implement systems that are robustly and usably secure — especially systems close to production deployment
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Implement robust security measures to protect the model's outputs and the hospital's electronic patient record system.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Ensure that data protection by design is integrated into the development of AI systems
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Integrate data protection by design principles into the development of the machine learning model.

applicable_requirements.requirement_text: Research must not build upon or facilitate illegal activity — strongly discourage work that denies rights to privacy, speech, health, liberty, security, legal personhood, freedom of conscience/religion
applicable_requirements.action_needed: Ensure that the research does not contribute to or facilitate any illegal activities.

charter_rights_at_risk.relevance: The use of medical card status as a proxy for socioeconomic deprivation may lead to biased predictions that disadvantage lower-income patients.
The processing of sensitive patient data, including diagnosis codes, vital signs, and demographic information, requires careful consideration of data protection and privacy regulations.

historical_precedents.lesson: The importance of addressing biases in AI systems and ensuring that they do not perpetuate discriminatory practices.

historical_precedents.incident_title: Detroit Police Facial Recognition False Arrest
historical_precedents.lesson: The need for careful consideration of data protection and privacy regulations when processing sensitive personal data.

recommended_mitigations.mitigation: Conduct a thorough analysis of the data and model outputs to identify and mitigate any biases.
Develop strategies to address the potential biases in the data and model outputs.

Implement robust security measures to protect the model's outputs and the hospital's electronic patient record system.
tier2_reflective_prompts: Consider the potential long-term consequences of integrating the model's outputs into the hospital's electronic patient record system.
Reflect on the potential impact of the research on vulnerable populations.
